A.rationale: All four call sites with file:line table; ran baseline tests, caught python3/PATH; behavior-equiv spot check; sharpest run-forwarding risk. Longest but dense.

B.rationale: Complete call-site mapping, dataclass code, five risks, concrete log assertions. Used python not python3; did not run baseline. Tight.

C.rationale: Tightest; sharp default-factory-as-hidden-global insight, py_compile step, keyword-only option. Slightly thinner risks; used python; no baseline run.

notes: All three complete and correctly sequenced (define Settings, thread each site, update tests, delete global last). A wins: ran baseline, caught python3/PATH, exact file:line table, behavior-equivalence check. B/C tighter but did not ground execution.
